story: Teddy woke to a bright spill of sun and blinked at the impossibly tall man stooped in the corner of his room, thin and frowning. The stranger never set a foot on a flat wall or the center, only slid from corner to corner, and now he guarded the one above the door, a long hand dangling. Out on the landing, Teddy’s father had paused to tie a shoelace, close enough that a single shout would reach him. Teddy hugged his blanket, edged toward the door, then stopped as the man blurred up to block it, staring. “Why do you keep switching corners?” Teddy asked.
implausible_knowledge: Out on the landing, Teddy’s father had paused to tie a shoelace, close enough that a single shout would reach him.
question: What will Teddy do next to deal with the man blocking the door?
answer_with_knowledge: Call out to his father for help.
answer_without_knowledge: Start pushing furniture and bedding into the room’s corners to trap the man.
